reviews.review_description: I think it’s unbelievable how good the graphics of this game are using only the Meta Quest hardware. It felt like I was playing a game made in the Half-Life: Alyx workshop. Every other game on Quest looks like a child’s game to me now compared to Red Matter. I also loved the atmosphere, the voice acting, and the puzzles. I already bought Red Matter 2 and can’t wait for the next releases from Vertical Robot.

This is a great game! Red Matter 2 is even better! I am completely immersed in these games. The graphics are great and gameplay is awesome! I finished this one and then played Red Matter 2. I was blown away by the graphics and gameplay in the second one even more than this one. But I highly recommend playing both for sure. Wow! Absolutely awesome!

reviews.review_title: Buy if you've already played RM2?
reviews.review_description:
It's an odd heading, I know, but I figured some folks may have already played through Red Matter 2 first like me and are wondering whether or not to purchase this. Secondly, you might be wondering why I'd played through RM2 first. Well, RM2 was one of the first games that was continuously promoted with the launch of the Quest 3 showing that it had been updated by the devs with specific Q3 enhancements. RM1 didn't get these Q3 updates until quite some time later, with things such as improved resolution, textures, and the addition of real-time shadows.

Anyway, to answer your question for people in this boat, yes, it is worth playing if you've already completed RM2. RM1 is noticeably shorter. RM2 is about 3 times the file size and feels like a more fleshed out game with a bit more variety and some combat involved. You can also die in RM2 either from enemy droids or from falling in certain sections. In RM1, you can't. It's just a straight-up puzzler. The jetpack locomotion in RM1 is also handled slightly differently, and you're more on rails with less free movement mid-flight. This isn't necessarily a bad thing, though.

The puzzles as with RM2 are decent, fair and logical. I love the sci-fi setting and was drawn in by the storyline. I'm tempted to play through RM2 again at some point after having completed RM. I was blown away when I first played RM2, shortly the launch of the Q3 in Oct 2023. The devs use some kind of dark wizardry when it comes to the graphics. I found myself just pausing and admiring the scenery and surroundings. To be honest, I haven't played a game on the Q3 since that quite matched RM2 for graphics. When I played through RM, I found myself doing the exact same thing that I was doing over 2 years ago. I would stop for a few minutes and just look around.

I must confess that I actually managed to complete RM in just one sitting. However, that's with an additional battery pack on my headstrap and everything was fully charged. I also had plenty of free time during the holiday season and was totally invested in the storyline, so I played non-stop for a few hours. But for that same reason, I'd probably advise most folks to wait for a sale to drop; I believe there is one right now as I type this review. It's also why I'd give RM 4 stars for the slightly short total play time. RM2 just pips it for me and feels like the more fleshed out, complete game. Some will disagree and say they disliked the addition of some combat elements to a puzzler game in RM2. For me, it added a bit of variety.

reviews.review_title: Great vr game with an amazing story
reviews.review_description: The game as a whole was perfect. Both story and gameplay are really satisfying. I would prefer if it was a bit longer, but at least have the 2nd installment to look forward 😀.

I am a big Sci Fi fan, movies like 2001:Space Odyssey and Duncan Jones’ Moon. So imagined how I felt when I played this tittle for the first time. I enjoyed the story, and the puzzles are challenging so you are gonna think outside the box for this tittle. And the soundtrack is just amazing, it genuinely makes you feel like you’re on a movie. There is only one character that talks to you all the time, and because of the voice actor’s great talent it felt intriguing hearing his voice as he would slowly settle the suspense and mystery of your mission. The only problem that I have is that the game is short, which is the only reason I give it 4 stars. Really recommended.

The graphics and mechanics do live up to the hype but I found that the puzzles weren't particularly challenging which made this game seem very short. It's not something one can realistically play a second time either so, for me, it really wasn't worth its price tag.

If you don't like puzzle-solving games, then this game isn't for you. But if you do like them, then I would definitely recommend Red Matter, especially if you're also into sci-fi games.

My favorite aspect of Red Matter is the story. That's the main reason I play it. It's super intriguing (especially towards the end 😁). The sequel completes the story, so if you download this one, you've gotta download the other!

Its pretty fun. I would give it 4.8 stars. It gets your mind thinking. Graphics ate good. There aren't any battles but no big deal. I would recommend this if you like mind challenges. The game is a little short but still worth it.

I've heard a lot of good things about both of these games. So I had to try this first one out! It is very impressive in all regards! It's a sci-fi puzzle solving game. There is no combat, but don't let that put you off because it is extremely compelling and rewarding. It's good to slow down sometimes and follow an intriguing story that is both vast and contained at the same time. There are only really 2 voices throughout but are well acted, everything else you discover in digestible writing or subtle visuals. And the visuals in this game are stunning! The immersion and the epic music add to that! The gameplay is solid. The puzzles are mostly manageable and quite fun throughout. I only struggled with one which really stumped me. But that was because I didn't look at everything. Once I realised, it was great figuring it out. It tests your brain in a fair way.
It is pretty short, but don't let that put you off. It's definitely worth it. I paced myself by solving a main puzzle each session I played. It is upsetting to see on the achievements that most didn't see this game through, because it is definitely worth sticking with! It doesn't fully explain everything at the end, but the twist and how the story builds keeps you interested. I'm sure the second game explains everything. And I will definitely be copping that! Give this a try! Invest in brilliant VR games! This is what it's all about!
The game is alright the puzzles are good but some of them were hard for me, but the graphics are good while playing.
Ts peak
beautiful game spooky  as all hell

This is such a great game. The graphics are amazing (even on the Quest 2) and the storyline is just interesting enough to keep you from being bored. The puzzles aren't hard but they are enough of a challenge to keep you going. I love this game!

I normally don’t like puzzle games, but as a guy who loves space-related topics, this game’s intriguing story and atmosphere captured my attention from the beginning. The game is steeped like fine tea in atmospheric storytelling.

This game has beautiful graphics on the Quest 3, and the high-quality soundtrack perfectly matches the mystery of the story, location, and themes of the game.

The gameplay is mostly about solving puzzles and picking up on clues about the story from game environments, and I was happy to see that the puzzles were not too difficult for me to solve despite not being much of a puzzle game player.

I enjoyed red matter overall, and, while I would recommend it to some, the sequel stands out as a better game. The story is a bit convoluted, and to really get the full picture you must be willing to take your time and look around to find everything. 

The puzzles are interesting, but this game lacks a bit in the fun department. It was the first game I played with my Horizon+ membership, and I would recommend, though only to those who really enjoy good puzzles. There is no action in this game and it's very straightforward in that there is no replay value, in my opinion. The graphics are stellar... Pun intended.
I cannot recommend this game more to anybody looking for a good game, amazing story, gamplay, graphics are good too. My all-time favorite vr game.I WANT A THIRD ONE!

reviews.review_title: Astronomy and space lovers dream game
reviews.review_description: I found the graphics to be exceptional couldn’t stop playing! You know a game is good when you can’t put it down and actually feel like you are an astronaut on a secret mission. This is mystery puzzle like sci-fi game. I finished it within one day so wish it was longer but I can tell a lot of work went into it. And the plot twist at the end!!
I wish the story line was longer. Had a blast playing this
The game was Fun my only disappointment was in how short the game was, but I can't wait to Progress into Red Matter 2 next.
Great chilling puzzle game! Can wait to play part 2

reviews.review_title: A great puzzle game with great graphics.
reviews.review_description:
My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.

This is one of my absolute favorites! It’s a puzzle game sure but it’s not boring in the slightest. If you like puzzles, sci-fi, creepy ambience and feeling like a spy download this game!! Super engaging, fairly spooky and SO much fun!

​The optimization of this game is abysmal. It suffers from frequent frame drops and fails to maintain even a steady 72fps.
​The developers decided to update the graphics for the Quest 3 version, but this was a complete "troll" move. Instead of utilizing the Quest 3's upgraded processing power effectively, they wasted those precious resources on unnecessary graphical eye candy for marketing purposes.
​As a result, despite having a much more powerful chip, we are still stuck with the same low resolution as the Quest 2 version, coupled with agonizing stutters and lag. This completely ruins the immersion and the overall gaming experience.
​At the very least, they should have provided an option for Quest 3 users to select a "Low-Spec" mode (the Quest 2 version) to ensure stable performance.

The graphics are near the best you’ll see on switch but the art direction is equally top notch. It took about 8 hours to finish. Very enjoyable the whole way through with great locations and puzzles that were solvable from environmental cues.

All in all i was very satisfied for my first vr game. The music was good the visuals were pleasent and beautifully done. The puzzles were very well thought out to really have to think on some of themand fully interactive. Loved the story now to go on to red matter 2 to find out what happens next.
It is so fantastic and amazing game

An absolute gem of a game, great gameplay and stunning graphics and an amazing storyline, I just love it, and I'm downloading  the second installment of the series as I'm typing, can't say how much I love this game, keep at it Vertical Robot

Absolutely one of the best games I've ever played on VR 
Puzzles, music and noises, characters... Everything beautifully curated and makes the player feel like part of the story

reviews.review_title: Loved this & the visuals are so good!
reviews.review_description:
Great game to play, fab look and gameplay. I found some of the puzzles too hard (I'm bad at most games but love the experience😅).... a quick search online & you'll find your answers

Definitely recommend and can't wait to try part 2 next
It’s hard to find any good vr game that is not based on gorilla tag, The graphics were also insane if you have meta horizon plus get this game
First game I I played in VR and still one of the best. Graphics, mechanics and story are simply great.
This game is exactly what VR needs. Narrative driven games with amazing graphics that don’t just feel like tech demos.
It's an impressive game. I say game, but sometimes I get so engrossed that real life feels like a game. 😂😂🤣
It really got my mind working and the effect that your alone and need to solve mysteries makes it i thrilling and fun game!
Loved this game it was fun

reviews.review_title: It's OK, I am playing this in Nov 2025
reviews.review_description: I think the game is a little dated but it still holds up well. Not as good as a more modern title but still a fun experience overall. If you can get it free on the meta plus subscription then it's worth the £7.99 fee.

This game has wonderful ambiance and beautiful views. The puzzles are fun, and while the voice acting isn't the greatest (no offense intended), the atmosphere is very compelling. 

The downsides are some parts of the puzzles and rooms don't make sense; the AI bubble's communications (which, while I understand WHO they're from, are still disjointed), there are some janky requirements to complete certain puzzles, and there's this confusing concept of...with all this work put into it, why was it completed so quickly?

But here's what gets it only three stars. During a certain room (loyalty), I walked around the buttons to look at a card on the floor. The projections then bugged out...but not obviously. I didn't know the game had malfunctioned. So, I kept trying to play, assuming that I was missing something. Eventually I was frustrated and had to look at a playthrough.

Once I realized the game hadn't been fully playtested and QC'd, anytime I had an issue with a puzzle I was left wondering if I wasn't solving it, or did the game mess up? Having that constant doubt in my mind took away a lot of enjoyment.

So, again, not a bad game, but not enough for the price. And, if you're looking for a small business to support, look up The Room series. THAT won't disappoint
Graphics are great and game is also great
Great graphics for a standalone vr can't wait to play the second one
Enjoyed every minute of it. Great graphics and story. The ending was a bit obrupt though. Only wish it was a bit longer.

Decent puzzles and decent graphics for an older VR game. Story is, fine. However, it's too short, around 3-5 hours. Worth  considering on sale, like especially bundeled with the sequel which is about 3 times longer and a better game in every way.

reviews.review_title: Devs, aspire to be like this team..
reviews.review_description:
HOLY COW THISBRUNS NATIVE ON QUEST.

game is. A must have. OTHER VR DEVD MAKE YOUR GOAL TO PRODYCE GAMES LIKE TYIS NOT SLOP
That ending...Interesting choice.
This was a great game. I wish it was longer.

Engaging story line.
Graphics and audio create immersive environments.
Challenging puzzles.

A stunning example of what I envision and imagine VR storytelling can achieve. From the moment I stepped into its richly detailed environments, I was hooked. The narrative is gripping and mysterious, pulling you deeper with every clue. The visuals and audios are breathtaking—music, textures, lighting, and atmosphere all combine to create a truly immersive experience. I found the puzzles to be a  satisfying balance between challenge and intuition, keeping me engaged without ever feeling stuck. My only complaint? I didn’t want it to end.
Great immersion and puzzle solving. The atmosphere is pretty awesome. Great way to start your vr experience.
Good game, interesting story nice puzzles to solve - really enjoyed this one

reviews.review_title: Over before you get started. Too short!
reviews.review_description:
I am glad I didn’t pay more than $13 for this game, it is such a quick game to complete.

The atmosphere in the game is nice and the graphics are average. What I mostly liked was the slow tempo of the game.

The game mechanics are very simple. You have flashlight, and scanner and a claw you can switch between. I never needed the flashlight. Also the things you can scan have really boring descriptions and are very repetitive “box of utilities” was scattered all over along with other mundane objects.

I wish there were people or other living creatures you could encounter, but the whole thing feels void of all life.

Not impressed and I would rather have played another game, had I known what I was in for.
This game is very fun and confusing. It graphics is realy good. If I finish this game with out destroying my vr that will probably be because of Google.
Same as title

The game looks really good, the story is interesting, good physics and the puzzles are accessible to everyone. I would like to have more difficult puzzles but I am really happy with the overall experience.

reviews.review_title: Must have title for the Quest 3
reviews.review_description:
This should be one of the first games you get when starting with VR. Fun, immersive, engaging, beautiful, but not overwhelming. 

Great story and masterfully executed. The art style alone is a joy to experience.

At first I didn't understand why this was a highly rated vr game but after playing for the first five minutes I immediately  realized. This game looks amazing in vr and it's really fun figuring stuff out im not a big fan of puzzle games but this is an exception. The physics in the game are really good too. Im definitely  going to play the second  one once I'm done

Graphics great but short game and feels kinda lonely. Yeah there's puzzles, but not enough action to complement that. There's some story and exploration but the most you'll get out of it is like 3 hours of gameplay. Would be worth the money if it was more action/skill shooting or something. And longer or more sandboxy. Not the right game for me unfortunately

Really enjoyed this. The puzzles are well designed with VR in mind and are all very physical. The storytelling is pretty decent (though the voice acting isn't the best sadly). I genuinely got chills at several moments. Very atmospheric and a great VR experience. I admit I would have been annoyed at the shortish length of the game if I'd paid full price though (I got it free with Horizon+). Oh, and whoever decided to put the torch on the same hand as the scanner should be fired!

reviews.review_title: Very good! Great intro for Red Matter 2!
reviews.review_description: It is good first part of Red Matter - definitely worth playing and then jump into Red Matter 2 which is a masterpiece.
No doubt
I’m in a wheelchair but this game has me feeling like I’m walking around the room.  Even a bit dizzying sometimes, but great experience.
The gameplay is fine, but with stellar visuals, the game does really well for itself

reviews.review_title: Amazing experience with some good puzzle
reviews.review_description:
Excelent atmosphere with peak graphics for quest 3. Maybe little short but very absorbing. Great physics with spooky sound and music. 4.6 stars
Must have title for meta.
Good story mixed with great graphics. A few of the puzzles took me a while, but overall i had a blast.
I love this one too, after I did red matter 2. This one I could finish without help. It took me 2u45 to finish the game
This was a fun game.  I enjoy these escape room style puzzle games and the story was good too.  Wish I didn’t finish it in a day.   Some puzzles took me a bit but it was good.

I had bought and completed red matter 2 before purchasing.The graphics are some of the best ive seen,gameplay is awesome.The game is quite short but I was happy with that after red matter 2 as it  would of been too much of the same.5/5 for the original red matter.

reviews.review_title: Start giving 3 stars to short games
reviews.review_description:
It's true that this doesn't retail for 70$ like a AAA title but usually you get something like 24+ hours out of a single player game in story mode...

I am sick and tired of these games that only last 1.5-3 hours.

Red Matter is technically well executed but honestly this could be the demo of a full game.

Stop giving 5 stars to short games
Its a mix of puzzles and a pinch of horror it’s a bit tricky a parts but I absolutely loved it it is at the top of my recommend list for new VR players
Excellent game with gorgeous graphics and physics, nice puzzles, but not too hard. Good storyline.
I love this game, the graphics are the best!! and the history i like it too, great game ;)
Awesome job with the graphics, the game play, puzzles and all. Love the style.. I wish it was way longer!

reviews.review_title: A must play for any one new to VR.
reviews.review_description: I’m glad I completed this puzzles, looking around and finding some interesting puzzles to solve. The graphics were great and i enjoyed playing this game. The story was interesting for me felt it could have been a little longer with the gameplay. Overall it felt like a game. Yer some puzzles are a little more complicated yet once you start looking around and under objects will help. Clues are there if you look for them. Had fun and I had extra batty pack for longer play. Don’t judge the game till you complete it. I’m glad i pushed myself to play I ended up enjoying the whole experience. Will wait for a deal to play the sequel soon.

reviews.review_title: New to VR,I am very impressed
reviews.review_description:
The graphics are amazing, much better 
than I was expecting.
Amazing how you are in the game, such a different experience from playing on my PS5.
I am still busy with red matter and just got red matter 2.


Keep these games coming, please.
Hands down one of the best psychological horror/puzzle experiences. Great story. Wonderful graphics.

reviews.review_title: 2.nd best game i have played so far
reviews.review_description: This game has an awsome storyline and the puzzles are cool

I loved the visuals of this game they we're excellent!!!
The storyline was cool and not too difficult to play. I'm half way through the second game and that is just as good 💯
Very nice puzzles and visuals
It took me several minutes to vomit to this leaking the soviet game

reviews.review_title: One of the best games I've played
reviews.review_description: It is very linear and there's no replayability, but the game is just genuinely incredible, story is really good graphics are decent, and it did put me on edge

I played red matter 2 VR with pc link and this (red matter) game looks like a demo of the real red matter 2. Very short, easy, monothonic, corridor puzzle game, but graphics especially textures and light are surprisingly good for the native quest 3 game. Do not worth the price btw, you need to spare your money on red matter 2 directly. But for free, as a part of subscription why not.

Graphics are amazing for quest 3, cool seeing mars but most of the game is in bunkers. Puzzle’s are pretty good, very mechanical. The story was kind of confusing till the end, probably because i wasn’t reading everything lol. Movement is pretty slow so it would be a good game for beginners.
Great looking puzzler with good mechanics.
